name: Digital Asset Summit (DAS) by Blockworks
edition: DAS: New York 2026 (institutional flagship; primary focus). Also in-window: DAS: London 2025.
location: Javits Center North, 445 11th Ave, New York, NY (DAS NYC 2026). DAS London 2025 was at Old Billingsgate, London, UK.
attendance: DAS NYC 2026: reported as 2,500+ attendees from 35+ countries, 150+ speakers, 45 curated sessions, with $4.2t+ in AUM represented (third-party listing). Blockworks/press materials cite $3t+ in AUM represented at past DAS events. DAS London 2025: hundreds of institutions / large audience representing ~£1.2t+ in AUM (third-party listing).
defi_themes: Onchain institutional finance and securities settlement (BlackRock, JPMorgan moving onchain to settle treasuries, equities, private credit and other securities)
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization of treasuries, equities, private credit and securities
Stablecoins and global payments infrastructure (institutional and consumer use cases)
DeFi protocol design, lending and borrowing markets, market structure
Risk curation and risk management in onchain credit / DeFi
Onchain trading venues and market structure
Digital asset regulation and US policy (post-landmark-legislation environment; SEC 'Project Crypto')
Institutional integration of digital assets into global capital markets
DeFi as collateral for institutional finance / on-chain treasuries

notable_panels.title: DeFi & Risk Management (track)
notable_panels.topic: Leading DeFi architects unpacking the evolution of DeFi: protocol design, lending and borrowing markets, market structure, risk curation, risk management, and onchain trading venues
notable_panels.speakers: Track-level theme confirmed across Blockworks event materials and recaps; individual panel speakers not individually verified

notable_panels.title: RWAs & Onchain Institutional Finance (track)
notable_panels.topic: How the largest financial institutions (e.g., BlackRock, JPMorgan) are moving onchain to settle treasuries, equities, private credit and other securities
notable_panels.speakers: Track-level theme; institutional participants include BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, Fidelity, JPMorgan, DTCC, Citi, Visa, Swift

notable_panels.title: Stablecoins & FinTech / Stablecoins and Global Payments (track)
notable_panels.topic: Evolution of global payments infrastructure and fintech; how stablecoins, vaults and embedded wallets are rewiring the financial stack
notable_panels.speakers: Devin McGranahan (Western Union); Paolo Ardoino (Tether); Richard Teng (Binance) among confirmed speakers in this thematic area

notable_panels.title: Tokenization's 'Trojan horse' moment (Day 2 session, per Blockworks recap)
notable_panels.topic: Argument that the industry must move from discussion to at-scale deployment of tokenization, citing a ~3-5 year 'execution window' before tokenization becomes 'table stakes'; tokenization's potential to transform access to US stocks/equities
notable_panels.speakers: Specific speaker names not reliably verified from accessible sources

notable_panels.title: BNB Chain case for RWA dominance (summit session/press)
notable_panels.topic: BNB Chain executives argued the network's user base and stablecoin activity give a structural edge for institutional tokenization, citing ~$1.5B in RWAs used as DeFi collateral and ~40% of global stablecoin volume
notable_panels.speakers: BNB Chain executives (names not individually verified)

notable_speakers.name: Paul S. Atkins
notable_speakers.affiliation: Chairman, U.S. Securities & Exchange Commission (headlined Day 1; architect of 'Project Crypto'; delivered opening remarks per SEC.gov)

notable_speakers.name: Samara Cohen
notable_speakers.affiliation: Global Head of Market Development, BlackRock

notable_speakers.name: Robin Vince
notable_speakers.affiliation: CEO, BNY (Bank of New York Mellon)

notable_speakers.name: Michael S. Selig
notable_speakers.affiliation: Chairman, U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC)

notable_speakers.name: Amy Oldenburg
notable_speakers.affiliation: Head of Digital Asset Strategy, Morgan Stanley

notable_speakers.name: Nigel Farage
notable_speakers.affiliation: MP (Clacton), Leader of Reform UK -- headlined Day 1 of DAS: London 2025 (Oct 13, 2025)

key_takeaways: DAS 2026 NYC was explicitly framed as the 'institutional era of crypto' summit -- the headline cast (SEC Chair Atkins, Fed Governor Miran, CFTC Chair Selig, BNY, BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, Tether, Binance) reflects deep TradFi + regulator engagement.

Regulation/policy was front and center: SEC Chairman Paul Atkins headlined Day 1 with a keynote on his vision for US digital-asset markets ('Project Crypto'); the agenda emphasized a post-landmark-legislation US/global policy environment.

Tokenization / RWA was the dominant DeFi-adjacent theme: panels centered on BlackRock and JPMorgan moving onchain to settle treasuries, equities, private credit and other securities; a Day 2 session framed tokenization as a 'Trojan horse' with a ~3-5 year execution window before it becomes 'table stakes.'

Stablecoins and global payments were a core pillar (dedicated Stablecoins & FinTech track), with Tether (Ardoino), Western Union (McGranahan) and Binance (Teng) among the voices on how stablecoins are rewiring payments infrastructure.

Institutional DeFi framing emphasized protocol design, lending/borrowing markets, market structure, risk curation and risk management -- positioning DeFi credit and onchain trading venues for institutional adoption rather than retail speculation.

RWA-as-collateral and chain competition: BNB Chain made a public case for RWA dominance, citing ~$1.5B in RWAs used as DeFi collateral and ~40% of global stablecoin volume, signaling competition among chains for institutional tokenization deals.

Content themes were consistent across DAS NYC 2026 and the in-window DAS London 2025: macro drivers of crypto, tokenization/RWA, stablecoins & payments, ETF/public-market adoption, DeFi & risk management, and evolving global regulation.
